elements.name: Description
texts: Knox describes Lough Derg as remote and obscure

elements.name: Source
texts: Pastoral Annals. By an Irish Clergyman [i.e. James S. Knox], p. 375

elements.name: Publisher
texts: R. B. Seeley and W. Burnside, London

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Google, sponsored by Princeton Library

elements.name: Text
texts: "In a lonely and remote region of the counties of Fermanagh and Donegal respectively, where scarcely a traveller ever passes, excepting those whom the sanctity of the spot allures, is situated the far-famed Lough Derg, with its celebrated island, containing Saint Patrick's Purgatory."
